titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Eardley Knollys Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 05:41

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Material is primariliy in English, with some material in French.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Norman Coates on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1894-2001. II. Writings, 1862-1954. III. Papers relating to Storran Gallery, 1894-1995. IV. Photographs, 1900-1999. V. Personal papers, 1892-2009.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Eardley Knollys Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Eardley Knollys papers contains correspondence, writings, personal and family papers, photographs, and business papers relating to Eardley Knollys and Storran Gallery. Material documents Knollys's relationships with writers, artists, poets, playwrights, and art critics.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Eardley Knollys Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Eardley Knollys
bioghist.p: Edward (Eardley) Knollys (born 1902, Alresford, Hampshire) was a British artist, art critic, and art dealer. In 1936, Knollys began working with Austrian art dealer Ala Story at her gallery, Storran Gallery in London. Story later sold her share of the business to Knollys, where he exhibited artists such as Monet, Renoir, Gauguin, Picasso, Vlaminck, Derain, Picasso and Modigliani. Following the end of World War II, and the death of Knollys' partner Frank Coombs, the gallery eventually closed.

Knollys, along with Eddy Sackville-West, and Desmond Shawe-Taylor, went on to purchase the Long Crichel House in Dorset to create an intellectual salon, hosting guests including Sybil Colefax, Anthony Asquith, Graham Sutherland, Lord Berners, Nancy Mitford, Benjamin Britten, Laurie Lee, Ben Nicolson, Cecil Day-Lewis, and Graham Greene.
Knollys was also an artist, who worked primarily in watercolor, and often featured rural Hampshire landscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One folder in Box 6, Series VI. Oversize.

did.unittitle: Pears: The Story of a Great Business , 1789-1924, 3 copies
Today: A Play in Four Acts by George Broadhurst and Abraham Schomer, adapted for the English stage by Eardley Knollys
Interview transcripts, art criticism essays, fiction stories, and other writings
Fry, Roger - Mayor and Clandac Galleries
Mitford, Nancy - Diary of a Russian Journey
Shum, Frederick - Reminiscences of the Late Thomas Barker
Photographs of the construction of studio at Long Crichel: prints
City of London School: prints and negatives
Storran Gallery artwork: prints and negatives
Biographical notes of the Knollys family 1367-1883 and other biographical information
Ceremony of the Knollys Rose: program and booklet
Royal Academy of Arts membership card
Ticket of admission to work at the British Gazette during general strike
